requirements.title: EPS SWC Architecture
requirements.description: SWC_ElectricPowerSteering shall be implemented as an AUTOSAR ApplicationSWC containing all torque assist computation and motor control logic for the electric power steering system
requirements.notes: Single SWC owns all EPS application logic

requirements.title: Control Torque Runnable
requirements.description: RE_ControlTorque shall execute cyclically every 5 ms to compute torque assist demand and write motor current output
requirements.notes: 5ms = 200 Hz control loop

requirements.title: Safety Monitor Runnable
requirements.description: RE_MonitorSafety shall execute cyclically every 10 ms to perform plausibility checks and update diagnostics
requirements.notes: 10ms safety monitor runs at half rate of control loop

requirements.title: Initialization Runnable
requirements.description: RE_Initialize shall execute once on ECU startup to validate sensor signals set internal state to INIT and perform RAM and ROM self-test
requirements.notes: Called by OS on AUTOSAR startup hook

requirements.title: Mode Change Runnable
requirements.description: RE_HandleModeChange shall execute on DataReceivedEvent of IgnitionStatus_CAN to perform mode transitions between INIT ACTIVE STANDBY FAIL_SAFE and SHUTDOWN
requirements.notes: Event-triggered on CAN signal change

requirements.title: Diagnostic Update Runnable
requirements.description: RE_DiagnosticUpdate shall execute on DataReceivedEvent of DEM notification to write active DTCs to NVM via NvM_WriteBlock
requirements.notes: Event-triggered by DEM callback

requirements.title: Required Ports
requirements.description: SWC shall declare: RP_VehicleSpeed (Sender-Receiver IF_SR_VehicleSpeed) RP_IgnitionStatus (SR IF_SR_IgnitionStatus) RP_YawRate (SR IF_SR_YawRate) RP_TorqueSensorPrimary (SR IF_SR_TorqueSensor) RP_TorqueSensorSecondary (SR IF_SR_TorqueSensor) RP_MotorTemperature (SR IF_SR_Temperature)
requirements.notes: All required ports follow AUTOSAR R-Port naming RP_

requirements.title: Provided Ports
requirements.description: SWC shall declare: PP_MotorCurrentDemand (Sender-Receiver IF_SR_MotorCurrent) PP_EPSStatus (SR IF_SR_EPSStatus) PP_AssistLevel (SR IF_SR_AssistLevel)
requirements.notes: All provided ports follow AUTOSAR P-Port naming PP_

requirements.title: Basic Torque Assist
requirements.description: The system shall compute a torque assist force proportional to the measured driver steering torque from TorqueSensorPrimary. Assist = driver_torque * base_gain
requirements.notes: Base gain is calibration parameter stored in ROM

requirements.title: Speed-Dependent Gain Reduction
requirements.description: Torque assist gain shall decrease linearly from base_gain at 0 km/h to 30% of base_gain at 40 km/h and continue decreasing to 10% of base_gain at 120 km/h
requirements.notes: Prevents oversteer at highway speeds

requirements.title: Minimum Assist at High Speed
requirements.description: At vehicle speeds >= 120 km/h torque assist shall not exceed 10% of rated maximum assist torque regardless of driver input
requirements.notes: Safety limit for high-speed stability

requirements.title: Maximum Assist at Low Speed
requirements.description: At vehicle speeds <= 5 km/h (parking maneuver) torque assist shall reach full rated assist capacity to minimize driver effort during parking
requirements.notes: Parking assist mode requires maximum torque amplification

requirements.title: Friction Compensation
requirements.description: The system shall add a friction compensation offset to the assist torque based on the rate of change of steering angle. Compensation = k_friction * d(angle)/dt
requirements.notes: Reduces steering effort during angle change phases

requirements.title: Active Return to Center
requirements.description: When the driver releases the steering wheel (driver torque < 0.5 Nm for > 200ms) the system shall apply a centering torque proportional to steering angle to return the wheel to center
requirements.notes: Improves straight-line driving comfort

requirements.title: End-Stop Protection
requirements.description: When the steering angle exceeds 90% of the mechanical end-stop limit the system shall reduce assist torque linearly to zero at 100% end-stop limit to prevent mechanical damage
requirements.notes: Prevents damage to steering rack at mechanical limits

requirements.title: Active Damping
requirements.description: The system shall apply an active damping torque opposing the rate of change of motor speed to prevent steering oscillations. Damping = k_damp * d(motor_speed)/dt
requirements.notes: Prevents shimmy and oscillation on rough roads

requirements.title: Road Camber Compensation
requirements.description: The system shall detect and compensate for steady-state torque offset caused by road camber by applying a slow-adapting offset cancellation filter with time constant 5s
requirements.notes: Optional comfort feature to reduce driver fatigue

requirements.title: Motor Current Calculation
requirements.description: The system shall convert the computed assist torque demand to a motor current setpoint using the motor torque constant Kt (Nm/A) stored as calibration parameter. I_motor = torque_demand / Kt
requirements.notes: Must account for motor efficiency curve at different speeds

requirements.title: Torque Demand Rate Limiter
requirements.description: The rate of change of total torque assist demand shall be limited to 500 Nm/s using a first-order rate limiter to prevent abrupt steering responses
requirements.notes: Rate limit is a key ASIL-D safety requirement

requirements.title: Low Speed Maneuvering Boost
requirements.description: At vehicle speeds below 10 km/h the system shall apply an additional gain factor of 1.5x to the computed assist torque to ease low-speed maneuvering and parking
requirements.notes: Parking boost improves user experience

requirements.title: High Speed Stability Assist
requirements.description: At vehicle speeds above 100 km/h the system shall apply a lane-keeping assist correction torque based on yaw rate deviation from expected value. Correction = k_hs * (yaw_measured - yaw_expected)
requirements.notes: Advanced feature requires yaw rate signal

requirements.title: Steering Angle Rate Limit
requirements.description: The rate of change of the commanded steering angle shall not exceed 720 deg/s to prevent mechanical shock loads on the steering rack
requirements.notes: Protects steering rack from high-speed angle transients

requirements.title: Torque Sensor Signal Processing
requirements.description: The driver torque sensor signal from RP_TorqueSensorPrimary shall be filtered using a 2nd-order Butterworth low-pass filter with 50 Hz cutoff to remove high-frequency noise before use in assist computation
requirements.notes: Noise rejection critical for stable control

requirements.title: System Operating Modes
requirements.description: SWC_ElectricPowerSteering shall implement the following operating modes managed by RE_HandleModeChange: INIT ACTIVE STANDBY FAIL_SAFE SHUTDOWN
requirements.notes: Mode is stored as internal state variable

requirements.title: Transition INIT to ACTIVE
requirements.description: The system shall transition from INIT to ACTIVE mode when: (a) all sensor signals are valid (b) supply voltage >= 9V (c) motor temperature < 80 degrees Celsius and (d) RAM/ROM self-test passed
requirements.notes: All conditions must hold simultaneously for exactly one evaluation cycle

requirements.title: Transition to STANDBY
requirements.description: The system shall transition to STANDBY mode when IgnitionStatus_CAN transitions from ON to OFF. Motor current output shall be ramped to zero over 500ms before entering STANDBY
requirements.notes: Graceful power-down prevents jerk on ignition off

requirements.title: Transition to FAIL_SAFE
requirements.description: The system shall immediately transition to FAIL_SAFE mode when any of the following conditions are detected: sensor plausibility failure motor current > 70A motor temp > 80C supply voltage < 9V watchdog timeout or CAN timeout > 50ms
requirements.notes: FAIL_SAFE transition must complete within 1 control cycle (5ms)

requirements.title: Transition to SHUTDOWN
requirements.description: The system shall transition to SHUTDOWN from any mode when ECU power is removed or when explicit shutdown command received. All outputs shall be set to zero before shutdown
requirements.notes: Power loss handled by hardware; SWC handles graceful shutdown signal

requirements.title: STANDBY Mode Behavior
requirements.description: In STANDBY mode the system shall hold motor current demand at zero. Sensor monitoring shall continue at reduced rate (10ms cycle). The SWC shall be ready to transition to ACTIVE within 100ms of ignition ON
requirements.notes: Sensor monitoring in STANDBY detects re-activation conditions

requirements.title: FAIL_SAFE Limp Home Mode
requirements.description: In FAIL_SAFE mode the system shall provide reduced assist at maximum 20% of rated capacity to allow the driver to complete the journey. Motor current limit shall be 14A (20% of 70A rated)
requirements.notes: Limp-home must be safe under all fault combinations

requirements.title: Mode Transition Timing
requirements.description: All mode transitions shall complete (outputs stabilized at new mode setpoints) within 50 ms of the triggering condition being detected
requirements.notes: Worst-case includes 10 control cycles at 5ms each

requirements.title: Torque Gradient Safety Limit
requirements.description: The rate of change of assist torque output shall never exceed 500 Nm/s. This limit is enforced in every execution of RE_ControlTorque independently of mode
requirements.notes: ASIL-D safety requirement - gradient limit prevents vehicle instability

requirements.title: Redundant Sensor Plausibility Check
requirements.description: RE_MonitorSafety shall compare TorqueSensorPrimary and TorqueSensorSecondary values every 10ms. If the absolute deviation exceeds 2.0 Nm for more than 3 consecutive cycles the system shall set DTC_EPS_SENSOR_FAIL and transition to FAIL_SAFE
requirements.notes: 3-cycle debounce prevents false positives from transient noise

requirements.title: Motor Current Hard Limit
requirements.description: Software shall limit motor current demand to <= 70A at all times. The hardware overcurrent protection triggers at 80A. If software limit is reached for > 100ms DTC_EPS_OVERCURRENT shall be set
requirements.notes: 10A margin between software and hardware limits provides safety layer

requirements.title: Thermal Protection
requirements.description: RE_MonitorSafety shall monitor RP_MotorTemperature every 10ms. When temp > 80 degrees Celsius assist shall be linearly reduced by 5% per degree above 80C. When temp > 100C motor shall be disabled and DTC_EPS_OVERHEAT set
requirements.notes: Linear derating prevents abrupt loss of assist

requirements.title: Supply Voltage Monitoring
requirements.description: RE_MonitorSafety shall monitor ECU supply voltage every 10ms. If voltage drops below 9.0V for more than 5 consecutive cycles assist shall be disabled and DTC_EPS_UNDERVOLTAGE set. System shall recover automatically when voltage returns above 10.5V
requirements.notes: Hysteresis between disable (9V) and recovery (10.5V) prevents chattering

requirements.title: Watchdog Supervision
requirements.description: The SWC shall trigger the hardware watchdog via Rte_Call every 5ms within RE_ControlTorque. A missed trigger shall cause the watchdog to reset the ECU. Software shall detect watchdog expiry via reset cause register on next startup
requirements.notes: Hardware watchdog is independent of software - ASIL-D requirement

requirements.title: CAN Signal End-to-End Protection
requirements.description: All safety-relevant CAN input signals (VehicleSpeed IgnitionStatus TorqueSensors) shall be protected by E2E Profile 2 as per AUTOSAR E2E library. E2E counter mismatch shall trigger DTC_EPS_CAN_TIMEOUT after 2 consecutive failures
requirements.notes: E2E protects against transmission errors on CAN bus

requirements.title: Startup Self-Test
requirements.description: RE_Initialize shall perform RAM checksum test and ROM CRC-32 verification on startup. Any failure shall set the SWC to FAIL_SAFE mode permanently until ECU is serviced
requirements.notes: ROM CRC computed offline and stored as calibration constant

requirements.title: Stack Overflow Detection
requirements.description: The OS configuration shall assign stack guard regions to all EPS tasks. Stack overflow shall trigger an immediate FAIL_SAFE transition and DEM error notification
requirements.notes: AUTOSAR OS stack monitoring feature

requirements.title: CPU Load Monitoring
requirements.description: RE_MonitorSafety shall measure execution time of RE_ControlTorque each cycle. If average CPU load exceeds 80% over 100 consecutive cycles a warning DTC shall be set and execution profiling data written to NVM
requirements.notes: CPU budget monitoring prevents real-time overload

requirements.title: DTC Sensor Failure
requirements.description: DTC_EPS_SENSOR_FAIL (0x910001) shall be reported to DEM via Dem_ReportErrorStatus when TorqueSensorPrimary and TorqueSensorSecondary disagree by > 2.0 Nm for 3 consecutive 10ms cycles
requirements.notes: DTC is ASIL-D classified - requires confirmed failure before set

requirements.title: DTC Overcurrent
requirements.description: DTC_EPS_OVERCURRENT (0x910002) shall be reported when motor current demand reaches the 70A software limit for more than 100ms. DTC shall be stored immediately in NVM
requirements.notes: Immediate NVM storage prevents data loss on power cycle

requirements.title: DTC Overheat
requirements.description: DTC_EPS_OVERHEAT (0x910003) shall be reported when motor temperature exceeds 80 degrees Celsius. DTC shall include snapshot data: temperature speed and assist level at time of fault
requirements.notes: Snapshot data aids field diagnostics

requirements.title: DTC Undervoltage
requirements.description: DTC_EPS_UNDERVOLTAGE (0x910004) shall be reported when supply voltage drops below 9.0V. DTC shall include voltage value snapshot
requirements.notes: Undervoltage most common EPS field complaint - snapshot critical

requirements.title: DTC CAN Timeout
requirements.description: DTC_EPS_CAN_TIMEOUT (0x910005) shall be reported when any safety-relevant CAN signal is absent or fails E2E check for more than 50ms (5 cycles at 10ms CAN cycle)
requirements.notes: CAN timeout must account for worst-case bus recovery time

requirements.title: DTC NVM Storage
requirements.description: All DTCs shall be stored in NVM via Dem_SetEventStatus followed by NvM_WriteBlock within the same RE_DiagnosticUpdate execution cycle to ensure fault persistence across power cycles
requirements.notes: NvM block ID for EPS DTCs is NvM_EPS_FaultLog

requirements.title: DTC Healing Condition
requirements.description: All healable DTCs (0x910001 through 0x910005) shall be cleared automatically after 3 consecutive fault-free evaluation cycles with no recurrence of the triggering condition
requirements.notes: Healing counter reset on each fault detection

requirements.title: Vehicle Speed CAN Input
requirements.description: VehicleSpeed signal shall be received on CAN via RP_VehicleSpeed with range 0-250 km/h resolution 0.01 km/h and a 10ms transmission cycle. Loss of signal > 50ms shall trigger DTC_EPS_CAN_TIMEOUT
requirements.notes: CAN message: VehicleStatusMsg ID 0x310

requirements.title: Ignition Status CAN Input
requirements.description: IgnitionStatus signal shall be received via RP_IgnitionStatus as a boolean (ON/OFF) with 20ms transmission cycle. Signal is E2E protected with Profile 2
requirements.notes: CAN message: PowerModeMsg ID 0x100

requirements.title: Yaw Rate CAN Input
requirements.description: YawRate signal shall be received via RP_YawRate with range -100 to +100 deg/s resolution 0.01 deg/s and 10ms transmission cycle for high-speed stability function
requirements.notes: CAN message: IMUDataMsg ID 0x420

requirements.title: Primary Torque Sensor Interface
requirements.description: TorqueSensorPrimary signal shall be received via RP_TorqueSensorPrimary from a direct SPI interface with range -25 to +25 Nm resolution 0.01 Nm and 1ms acquisition cycle. Signal shall be filtered before use
requirements.notes: SPI sensor provides raw ADC value - conversion to Nm in SWC

requirements.title: Secondary Torque Sensor Interface
requirements.description: TorqueSensorSecondary shall provide a fully redundant torque measurement via RP_TorqueSensorSecondary with identical range and resolution to the primary sensor but using an independent SPI bus and different physical sensor element
requirements.notes: Redundancy requires physical and electrical independence from primary

requirements.title: Motor Current Output
requirements.description: MotorCurrentDemand shall be written via PP_MotorCurrentDemand with range 0-70A resolution 0.1A and updated every 5ms in RE_ControlTorque. The motor controller ECU reads this signal via CAN to command the motor drive
requirements.notes: CAN message: EPSControlMsg ID 0x520

requirements.title: EPS Status CAN Output
requirements.description: EPSStatus shall be written via PP_EPSStatus every 10ms containing: current_mode (4 bits) active_dtc_count (8 bits) assist_level_percent (8 bits) and system_health (4 bits). This data is used by the instrument cluster and gateway
requirements.notes: CAN message: EPSStatusMsg ID 0x530
